source: Harry Potter series by J.K. Rowling

memories.content: Harry Potter discovered he was a wizard when Rubeus Hagrid burst through the door of the Dursleys' cabin on Privet Drive, smashing it off its hinges, and handed him his Hogwarts acceptance letter on his eleventh birthday.
Harry received his first wand from Ollivander's shop on Diagon Alley - eleven inches, holly and phoenix feather, the same wand that gave Voldemort his power seventeen years earlier.
The goblin Griphook led Harry through the hidden vaults of Gringotts Bank, including the spectacular vault containing the Philosopher's Stone, protected by numerous enchantments.
Harry met Draco Malfoy on the Hogwarts Express, who tried to befriend him but Harry refused after Draco insulted Hagrid and mocked Ron Weasley.
The Sorting Hat was placed on Harry's head and debated between Gryffindor and Slytherin before Harry begged not to be put in Slytherin, and the Hat placed him in Gryffindor.
Hermione Granger corrected Professor Snape when he mispronounced a potion ingredient, earning his instant and lasting enmity throughout their school years.
Neville Longbottom's Remembrall flashed bright red when he couldn't remember where he left his password Remembrall, showing his famous forgetful nature from the very first day.
Quirinus Quirrell caught Harry, Ron, and Hermione out of bed on Halloween night when a troll had somehow entered the castle, though Professor Quirrell claimed he had already dealt with it.
Harry caught his first Golden Snitch in his mouth during his first Quidditch match against Draco Malfoy, earning him a spot on the Gryffindor Quidditch team as Seeker.
Harry's Firebolt broomstick was confiscated by Professor McGonagall after arriving mysteriously without a note, causing Hermione to suspiciously report it to Professor McGonagall.
Hermione convinced Professor McGonagall to return Harry's Firebolt after Harry, Ron, and herself discovered that Norbert the Norwegian Ridgeback was a dragon being secretly raised in Hagrid's hut.
Charlie Weasley and his friends flew in from Romania in the middle of the night to take Norbert the dragon, and the three friends barely made it back to the tower before being caught.
Hermione Granger punched Draco Malfoy in the face when he called Ron a stupid git after Ron's lost rat Scabbers was found to be alive.
Argus Filch caught Harry, Ron, and Hermione outside the library corridor when they were searching for Nicolas Flamel's identity, dragging them to Professor McGonagall.
Harry received an Invisibility Cloak from Dumbledore on Christmas night, a gift that had belonged to Harry's father James, with a note saying 'I thought you might like to use it.'
Harry discovered the Mirror of Erised hidden behind a curtain in a room on the forbidden third floor, seeing his dead parents and whole family reflected back at him whenever he stood before it.
Harry found Nicolas Flamel after Dumbledore let him work out the riddle from the envelope Hermione received about the philosopher's stone and his chocolate frog card.

Hermione Granger solved the potions puzzle on the way to the Philosopher's Stone by correctly identifying that the second bottle contained poison while the third bottle would only allow one person forward.
Harry confronted Professor Quirrell in the final chamber, discovering that Quirrell was the one trying to steal the Philosopher's Stone and that his turban actually concealed Voldemort's face.

Voldemort tried to possess Harry's body to reach the Philosopher's Stone, but Harry's love and mother's sacrifice protected him, causing Voldemort's essence to be destroyed and Quirrell to crumble to dust.

Harry awoke in the hospital wing after being knocked unconscious by Voldemort, and Professor Dumbledore revealed the Mirror of Erised's true nature and that only someone wanting to find but not use the Stone would get it.
The Weasley twins enchanted the passwords to the dormitories to change every Saturday, and Fred and George showed Harry all the secret passages they had discovered.
Voldemort asked Professor Quirrell to help him get the Philosopher's Stone in exchange for allowing him to teach at Hogwarts, turning Quirrell into his willing servant.
Lily Evans blocked Voldemort three times when he tried to kill Harry, her love creating a protection that followed Harry even to the Dursleys' house.
Harry first stepped onto Hogwarts grounds when Hagrid rowed him across the lake on the night before Sorting, seeing the castle lit up against the dark sky.
The Hogwarts Express departed from Platform Nine and Three-Quarters at precisely 11 AM on September 1st, carrying Harry to his new life as a wizard.
Dobby appeared to Harry in his bedroom at the Dursleys' house, warning him about returning to Hogwarts and punishing himself by slamming his head against the wall.
Harry's scar burned whenever Voldemort was feeling strong emotions, including during the confrontation with Quirrell when his scar was described as feeling like it was split open.
Nicolas Flamel was the ancient alchemist who created the Philosopher's Stone, allowing him and Dumbledore to live for over six hundred years before the Stone was destroyed.
Florean Fortescue gave Harry extra ice cream whenever he visited Diagon Alley, showing kindness to the young wizard and later disappearing under mysterious circumstances.
The troll that entered Hogwarts on Halloween was actually let in by Quirrell as part of Voldemort's plan to distract the teachers while he went for the Stone.
Harry saw his parents in the Mirror of Erised every time he looked into it, a vision that gave him both comfort and deep sadness about the family he had lost.
Professor McGonagall turned into a cat on the first day of class to demonstrate transfiguration, surprising all the first-years including Harry.
Hagrid accidentally let slip that Fluffy the three-headed dog was guarding something important, giving Harry and his friends a clue about the Stone's protection.
Neville won ten points for Gryffindor by standing up to Harry, Ron, and Hermione when they were breaking rules, showing his bravery despite his clumsiness.
The Devil's Snare plant in the underground chamber nearly strangled Harry and Hermione until Hermione remembered that the plant disliked fire and freed them.
The Mirror of Erised showed people their deepest desires, and only someone who wanted to find but not use the Stone could obtain it from the mirror's depths.
Harry and Ron became best friends on the Hogwarts Express when Ron shared his sandwiches with Harry and they defeated the troll together in the bathroom.
Hermione became friends with Harry and Ron after they saved her from the troll in the bathroom, proving herself as a loyal companion despite her know-it-all attitude.
Dobby the house-elf came to warn Harry about the Chamber of Secrets being opened and was severely punished by the Malfoys for speaking against his masters.
The potion created by Nicolas Flamel could turn any metal into gold and produce the Elixir of Life, extending the drinker's lifespan indefinitely.
Harry was the only Gryffindor Seeker in a century, a remarkable achievement that made the team competitive in the house Quidditch Cup.
The Sorting Hat warned the Great Hall that Slytherin would help someone gain the Stone, foreshadowing Quirrell's betrayal and Voldemort's return.
Neville Longbottom broke his wrist when he fell from his broomstick during Quidditch practice, sent flying by Madame Hooch's whistle that surprised him.
Harry practiced flying with Wood every evening before Quidditch matches, learning advanced maneuvers that would help him catch the Golden Snitch.
The Mirror of Erised was moved to Dumbledore's office after Harry told him about it, placed behind a curtain where it remained for years.
Voldemort's spirit fled Godric's Hollow after his Killing Curse rebounded, possessing Quirrell for two years before attempting to steal the Philosopher's Stone.
Harry attended the End-of-Term Feast where Gryffindor won the House Cup with the last-minute points awarded by Dumbledore for standing up to Quirrell.
Harry accidentally inflated Aunt Marge by blowing too much air into her when she insulted his parents during a dinner at the Dursleys' house, forcing him to flee to the Burrow.
Ron Weasley accidentally drove the bewitched Ford Anglia flying the Weasley brothers had given him, crashing into the magical Whomping Willow in the dark of night.
Professor Lockhart accidentally petrified Harry when he tried to use Ron's broken wand that backfired when they were attacked by Cornish pixies in his Defense Against the Dark Arts class.
memories.location: Defense Against the Dark Arts Classroom, Hogwarts

memories.content: Harry heard a mysterious voice coming from the walls of Hogwarts saying 'There will be no more Mudbloods at Hogwarts,' a voice that only he and Ron could hear while they were on their way to Moaning Myrtle's bathroom.
Mrs. Norris was petrified by a giant snake called Basilisk that lived within the pipes of Hogwarts, found hanging from a torch bracket by Filch with a note reading 'PIPING PETRIFYING PUPILS.'

Justin Finch-Fletchley was petrified when the Basilisk came through the pipes in the corridor where Filch caught Harry after the Writing on the Wall appeared announcing the Chamber of Secrets was open.
Nearly Headless Nick was petrified by the Basilisk when he passed in front of a bathroom mirror that reflected the serpent's eyes instead of directly looking at it through the pipes.
Hermione was petrified in the library when she found a piece of parchment about the Chamber of Secrets with a description of the Basilisk that she had been researching before being frozen.
Colin Creevey was petrified when he snuck into the school despite his Muggle-born parents' worried letters, found in a corridor with his camera pointed toward something he had tried to photograph.
Penelope Clearwater was petrified while trying to hide from the monster with her boyfriend Percy, found in the library corridor along with her shattered mirror showing the Basilisk's reflection.
Dobby revealed that the Chamber of Secrets had been opened fifty years ago by the Heir of Slytherin, and that he was being forced to prevent Harry from returning to Hogwarts by any means necessary.
Ginny Weasley was being possessed by Tom Riddle's memory through her diary, writing in blood on the walls and slowly becoming more pale and withdrawn as the year progressed.
Harry found the diary of Tom Riddle in Ginny's belongings and when he was possessed by the memory inside it, he saw a vision of the original opening of the Chamber of Secrets fifty years ago.
Moaning Myrtle drowned when the Basilisk emerged from the toilet she was crying in, fifty years before she guided Harry to the entrance of the Chamber of Secrets through her bathroom.
Harry and Ron entered the Chamber of Secrets through the hidden pipes after following Ginny's location through Parseltongue, using the beaded Riddle diary to guide them to the underground lake.
Tom Riddle manifested fully from the diary and revealed he was Voldemort as a teenager, explaining how he framed Hagrid for opening the Chamber fifty years ago when it was actually him.
Fawkes the phoenix was sent by Dumbledore to rescue Harry in the Chamber of Secrets, bringing the Sorting Hat that contained the Sword of Gryffindor with a basilisk fang enchanted inside it.
Harry stabbed the Basilisk through the roof of its mouth with the Sword of Gryffindor that he pulled from the Sorting Hat, killing the serpent and collecting several of its venomous fangs.
Harry destroyed the Horcruxe diary by stabbing it with a Basilisk fang, causing Ginny to be released from the diary's control and Tom Riddle's memory to be permanently destroyed.
Harry asked Dumbledore to employ Dobby as a free elf at Hogwarts, but Lucius Malfoy stormed in furious about the destroyed diary and Dobby was sent away, later working at Hogwarts kitchen.
Harry missed the Hogwarts Express and had to fly the enchanted Ford Anglia to school, crashing into the Whomping Willow on their arrival at the castle.
Lockhart claimed he had written every book in the library about his own adventures, though most were exaggerated or completely fabricated versions of other wizards' achievements.
Ginny Weasley wrote terrible poetry about Harry in her diary, revealing her deep crush on him before the diary's dark influence began to consume her personality.
The Whomping Willow was planted in 1970s to hide the entrance to the tunnel leading from the Shrieking Shack to the Hogwarts grounds.
Filch searched the corridors for the Heir of Slytherin with his cat Mrs. Norris, growing increasingly paranoid about Mudbloods being in danger.
Moaning Myrtle complained about being excluded and cried in her bathroom for years before Harry and Ron discovered she had been the first victim of the Basilisk.
Ginny Weasley was dragged into the Chamber of Secrets by the pull of the diary, her consciousness fading as Tom Riddle gained more power.
Harry used Parseltongue to open the Chamber of Secrets sink in Moaning Myrtle's bathroom, commanding the ancient snake-themed pipes to reveal the entrance.
Tom Riddle asked Harry how he planned to stop him without using the sword, confident that Harry would be unable to destroy him with the Basilisk already gone.
The Sword of Gryffindor was stored inside the Sorting Hat and could only be retrieved by a true Gryffindor when they truly needed it.
Fawkes's tears could heal wounds as well as his flames could burn, and his appearance signaled that Dumbledore was watching over Harry's safety.
Ginny recovered completely once the diary was destroyed, her personality returning to normal as Tom Riddle's possession was lifted from her mind.
Lucius Malfoy planted the diary in Ginny's belongings hoping it would reopen the Chamber and cause chaos, though he didn't know it was a Horcruxe.
Percy Weasley proved his loyalty to authority by reporting his own brothers to Professor McGonagall when he suspected them of involvement in the reopening of the Chamber.
Harry used the Marauder's Map for the first time to navigate the secret passages of Hogwarts, discovering hidden routes that would become essential to his adventures.
Fred and George Weasley gave Harry and Ron the Marauder's Map that they had borrowed from Filch's office, having discovered its secret phrases to activate it.
Harry met Moaning Myrtle properly for the first time when he visited her bathroom to investigate the voice he had been hearing about Mudbloods.
The enchanted car rescued Harry and Ron from the frozen lake in the Forbidden Forest, driving itself back to the Weasley home with the unconscious boys inside.
Harry was caught by Filch near the Writing on the Wall, accused of being the Heir of Slytherin until Professor McGonagall intervened on his behalf.
Harry found that he could speak Parseltongue because a fragment of Voldemort's soul was inside him from the night his parents were killed.
The Basilisk had been living in the pipes of Hogwarts for a thousand years, emerging only when the Heir of Slytherin commanded it to purge the school of Muggle-borns.

Harry discovered he was illegally expelled from Hogwarts when the Ministry sent a Howler demanding he be allowed back to school immediately after Uncle Vernon refused to sign the Hogsmeade permission form.
Harry learned that his godfather Sirius Black had allegedly betrayed his parents to Voldemort and murdered twelve Muggles, escaping from Azkaban where the Dementors guarded him.
Harry's Firebolt was inspected by Madam Hooch and later cleared by Ollivander who confirmed it had no dark magic curses despite Hermione's concerns about its safety.
Professor Trelawney predicted Harry's death during her first Divination class, claiming that the Dark Servant would return before the night ended and that Harry would 'cross paths with death.'
Harry's Nimbus Two Thousand was destroyed when the Knight Bus rammed into the Whomping Willow while swerving to avoid hitting Harry on his way to Hogsmeade.
Harry discovered the Marauder's Map in his birthday present from Fred and George Weasley, a magical map showing everyone in Hogwarts and all its secret passages.
Hermione signed up for every possible class at Hogwarts including Time-Turner lessons, creating a schedule so packed she barely had time to eat or sleep.
Harry saw Sirius Black enter the Honeydukes basement through the whomping willow's passage and tried to chase him through the tunnel to Hogsmeade.
Scabbers the rat bit Peter Pettigrew disguised as Ron Weasley and escaped, leading to Ron's devastating loss of his beloved pet rat of twelve years.
The Fat Lady portrait was slashed and ruined when Sirius Black apparently broke into the castle looking for something, causing enhanced security measures including Filch's new patrol routes.
Harry received the Invisibility Cloak from Dumbledore at the end of last year with a note saying to use it well, which he now used to explore the castle at night.
Ron was attacked by Sirius Black in his dormitory and nearly killed when Black crept in with a knife, but Professor McGonagall arrived just in time to save Ron and Pettigrew escaped.
Harry and Hermione used the Time-Turner to rescue Buckbeak the Hippogriff from execution and went back three hours to save him from the executioner's axe.
Harry watched from the window as Sirius Black was about to be kissed by the Dementor, realizing that Buckbeak could save him if Hermione had already rescued the Hippogriff.
The Dementors forced Harry to hear his parents' dying screams when they searched the grounds for Sirius Black, causing Harry to collapse and faint during the Quidditch match.
Professor Lupin taught Harry the Patronus Charm to defend against Dementors, explaining that only a very strong happy memory could produce a fully corporeal Patronus.
memories.location: Defense Against the Dark Arts Classroom, Hogwarts

memories.content: Harry produced a fully corporeal stag Patronus on the lake shore to save himself and Sirius Black from a hundred Dementors, the most powerful defensive magic a wizard could produce.
Sirius Black revealed he was innocent and that Peter Pettigrew was the real traitor who had been hiding as Ron's pet rat Scabbers for twelve years.
Peter Pettigrew was unmasked by Ron when his mangled finger revealed the scar that he had lost, showing the missing piece of the story when he cut off his own finger to fake his death.
Snape nearly killed Sirius Black by pulling back his shirt to show the Dark Mark while screaming that Black had murdered James and Lily Potter.
Harry discovered that his father James had also used the Marauder's Map and had bullied Snape during their school years, causing Harry to feel ashamed of his father.
Dumbledore explained to Harry that only someone with a time-traveling device could survive that night, giving Hermione the Time-Turner with strict instructions about the rules.
Sirius Black escaped on Buckbeak the Hippogriff through the forest after the Ministry realized he was innocent, disappearing into the wilderness with Dumbledore's blessing.
Professor Lupin resigned from teaching when the werewolf transformation would have put students at risk, leaving the Defense Against the Dark Arts teaching post vacant once again.
Harry learned the true identity of his godfather when Dumbledore revealed that Sirius Black was indeed James and Lily's close friend and secret keeper before their deaths.
Harry sent Sirius Black the Firebolt as a Christmas present through Professor McGonagall to help his godfather stay hidden and comfortable while on the run.
James Potter had been an Animagus who could transform into a stag, giving Harry his Patronus form and the nickname Padfoot for his dog form.
Peter Pettigrew betrayed James and Lily Potter to Voldemort by confirming their location after being promised power and protection from the Dark Lord.
Buckbeak the Hippogriff attacked Malfoy when he provoked it during Hagrid's lesson, nearly injuring Draco's arm and leading to Buckbeak's death sentence.
Harry used the Marauder's Map to track Peter Pettigrew's location in the castle, realizing that Scabbers was not what he appeared to be.
Luna Lovegood's mother Pandora died when she was nine years old while experimenting with spells in the garden, a tragedy that made Luna believe in the supernatural.
Harry was rescued by the Knight Bus after Dementors attacked him and Dudley in Little Whinging, the triple-decker purple bus materializing around them.
Harry learned that his father James had saved Snape from becoming a werewolf by pulling him back from the Willow, though Snape never forgave James for this.
Hermione punched Draco Malfoy in the Three Broomsticks when he mocked Harry about his godfather being a murderer, defending her friend's honor.
Harry's name mysteriously came out of the Goblet of Fire during the Triwizard Tournament selection ceremony, making him the fourth champion despite being underage.
Barty Crouch Junior disguised as Alastor Moody took Harry's Imperiused classmate and used Polyjuice Potion to maintain his fake Moody appearance throughout the year.
Harry taught Cedric Diggory how to get past the Hungarian Horntail by showing him where the dragon's golden egg was hidden, repaying an earlier favor from Cedric.
The golden egg revealed Harry's next task involved underwater creatures when opened underwater, giving him the clue that he would need to rescue something precious from the lake.
Harry used the Summoning Charm to bring his Firebolt during the first task when facing the Hungarian Horntail, summoning it to catch his key that fell from his pocket.
Moody demonstrated the Unforgivable Curses to the fourth-year class including the Cruciatus Curse, imperiling Neville's safety by making him feel the effects when he looked away.
memories.location: Defense Against the Dark Arts Classroom, Hogwarts

memories.content: Harry won the second task in the lake by using Gillyweed to breathe underwater for an hour, arriving just in time to save not only Ron but also Fleur's little sister Gabrielle.
Moody told Harry to plant his name in the Goblet of Fire, convincing him through persistent suggestions that he could win the tournament despite the danger.
Dobby the house-elf warned Harry about the champion's plot against him when he overheard the servants talking about the tournament being rigged.
Viktor Krum questioned Harry about what it felt like to touch a live dragon, revealing his nervousness about the upcoming task before he himself competed.
Fleur Delacour confronted Harry after the second task, telling him that his rescue of Gabrielle was braver than anything she had seen in her life.
Moody's ExcitementSpell made Harry feel the Dark Mark burning on his forehead during the third task, causing confusion and suspicion about what was happening.
Harry entered the Triwizard maze as the last champion and navigated through it using the Marauder's Map to avoid the many enchanted obstacles placed throughout.
Cedric Diggory was killed by Peter Pettigrew on Voldemort's orders in the graveyard behind the Riddle house, his body left for Harry to find.
Voldemort's rebirth ceremony used his father's bones, Wormtail's hand, and Harry's blood to create a new body, making the ritual particularly significant and personal.
Harry dueled Voldemort in the graveyard and their wand cores connected, causing the Priori Incantatem effect that produced ghostly echoes of everyone Voldemort had killed.
Harry's parents emerged as Priori Incantatem ghosts from Voldemort's wand and gave Harry enough time to escape by telling him to grab Cedric and fly to Hogwarts.
Harry returned Cedric's body to Hogwarts and told the Ministry officials that Voldemort had returned, but they refused to believe him despite the Triwizard evidence.
The Triwizard Tournament ended in tragedy when Cedric Diggory was killed and Harry returned with his body, the Ministry denying all claims about Voldemort's return.
Moody's real body was discovered in his enchanted trunk by Dumbledore and Fudge, revealing that Barty Crouch Junior had impersonated him the entire year.
Wormtail prepared the potion for Voldemort's rebirth using bone, blood, and flesh in a cauldron over a dark fire surrounded by Inferi waiting in the waters.
The Ministry of Magic Floo Network was buzzing with rumors about Harry's claims of Voldemort's return, causing panic among the magical community.
The champions' names burned in fire above the Goblet of Fire when Harry's name emerged as the fourth champion, shocking everyone present in the Great Hall.
Ludo Bagman placed bets on the Triwizard Tournament and owed the goblins money, leading to his downfall when he fled the country after the tournament.
Bertha Jorkins was killed by Voldemort after he extracted information about the Triwizard Tournament and Barty Crouch Junior from her memory.
Harry's scar burned whenever Voldemort was feeling strong emotions, including during the rebirth ritual when his scar was described as feeling like it was split open.
Dementors attacked Harry in Little Whinging after Dudley and his gang cornered Harry, causing Harry to produce a stag Patronus to save both himself and his cousin from the creatures.
Harry was rescued by the Knight Bus and taken to the Burrow, where he learned that the Ministry had thrown him out of school for using magic outside school.
The Order of the Phoenix members including Remus Lupin and Tonks guarded Harry from Privet Drive, using the Floo Network and advanced protection charms to keep him hidden.
Harry was expelled from Hogwarts for using magic outside school and faced a disciplinary hearing before the full Wizengamot, where Dumbledore testified on his behalf.
The Ministry of Magic claimed through the Daily Prophet that Harry was lying about Voldemort's return, using their media influence to discredit him as mentally unstable.
Dolores Umbridge was appointed as the new Defense Against the Dark Arts professor by the Ministry, bringing her signature black quill that cut words into students' skin.
memories.location: Defense Against the Dark Arts Classroom, Hogwarts

memories.content: Harry founded Dumbledore's Army in the Hog's Head Inn, a secret study group where he taught real Defense Against the Dark Arts to students who wanted to learn practical magic.
Cho Chang kissed Harry in the Room of Requirement after a DA meeting, marking the beginning of their brief and complicated romantic relationship.
Sirius Black gave Harry the two-way mirror that had once belonged to James, telling him to use it if he ever needed to contact him directly.
Harry experienced a disturbing vision of Arthur Weasley being attacked by a giant serpent in the Department of Mysteries, the first clear vision he'd had of Voldemort's actions.
Neville's grandmother Augusta Longbottom showed him the memory of his parents being tortured into insanity by Bellatrix Lestrange, inspiring Neville to join Dumbledore's Army.
Harry accidentally broke Ginny's arm when he lost control of his emotions during a Quidditch match, causing his Firebolt to spin wildly and knock her from her broom.
Marietta Edgecombe betrayed Dumbledore's Army to Dolores Umbridge, earning herself permanent acne across her face from the charm Hermione had placed on the membership list.
Dolores Umbridge forced Harry to write with her blood-quill as punishment, carving 'I must not tell lies' repeatedly into his own hand in detention.
Fred and George Weasley performed their spectacular Exit Ominously fireworks across Hogwarts on the day they left school, creating chaos in the O.W.L. exam.
Harry realized through Occlumency lessons with Professor Snape that his visions of Voldemort were actually two-way mental connections, not just one-sided dreams.
Snape's worst memory revealed to Harry through Occlumency lessons showed James bullying Snape on the Hogwarts Express and Harry feeling ashamed of his father.
Harry saw through Voldemort's vision that Sirius Black was being tortured in the Department of Mysteries, realizing it was a trap to lure him there.
Harry led Dumbledore's Army to the Department of Mysteries to save Sirius, arriving only to find that the vision was a trap set by Voldemort himself.
Neville fought bravely in the Department of Mysteries, holding off Death Eaters with Luna when everyone else was separated and vulnerable.
Bellatrix Lestrange tortured Neville's parents Frank and Alice Longbottom into madness using the Cruciatus Curse, a trauma that haunted Neville throughout his childhood.
The prophecy about Harry and Voldemort was originally made by Sybill Trelawney in 1980, predicting that one must die at the hand of the other.
Dumbledore arrived at the Ministry just in time to save Harry and the DA members from Voldemort, engaging in a spectacular duel with him in the Ministry Atrium.
Voldemort possessed Harry during the battle in the Ministry, trying to convince Harry to let him use his body to retrieve the prophecy, but Harry refused.
Bellatrix Lestrange escaped from Azkaban prison in the chaos of the battle, the only Death Eater to ever break out of the maximum security wizarding prison.
Sirius Black was killed by Bellatrix Lestrange's curse in the Department of Mysteries Archway room, falling through the ancient Veil and becoming trapped between life and death.
Harry screamed Sirius's name in grief after watching him fall through the Veil, the loss of his godfather causing him the worst pain he had ever experienced.
Harry realized through the two-way mirror that he could have saved Sirius if he had used it properly, the guilt of not thinking of the gift nearly destroying him.
The Daily Prophet printed the truth about Voldemort's return after the battle at the Ministry, finally admitting that Fudge and the Ministry had been wrong about Harry.
Mr. Weasley was attacked by the snake Nagini at the Ministry of Magic, receiving a deep bite on his leg that nearly killed him before being saved.
Harry saw his parents in the Mirror of Erised every time he looked into it, a vision that gave him both comfort and deep sadness about the family he had lost.
Hagrid's giant half-brother Grawp was discovered in the Forbidden Forest when Hagrid was trying to hide him from the Ministry after the giants were persecuted.
The prophecy was stored in the Department of Mysteries, a circular room filled with hundreds of prophecy orbs that could only be touched by the people they concerned.
Dumbledore arrived at the Dursleys' house to escort Harry personally to the Burrow, explaining that he needed Harry's help with important work for the Ministry.
Harry accidentally set his aunt's curtains on fire when Dudley's gang cornered him, the accidental magic showing how emotionally volatile his magic had become.
Harry discovered the Half-Blood Prince's textbook in the back of the bathroom where it had been abandoned, a book annotated with helpful spells and dark magic annotations.
Harry used the Half-Blood Prince's spell Sectumsempra against Draco Malfoy in the bathroom, nearly killing him before Professor Snape arrived and saved Draco's life.
Professor Slughorn pretended he hadn't been told about Horcruxes when Dumbledore visited him, but the memory he gave Harry after being plied with Felix Felicis was actually tampered with.
Harry used Felix Felicis to convince Slughorn to give him the true memory about Horcruxes, drinking the liquid luck potion that made everything go perfectly for one day.
Dumbledore revealed to Harry that Voldemort had split his soul into Horcruxes hidden throughout the world, making him immortal unless all the Horcruxes were destroyed.
Harry and Dumbledore found the fake Horcruxe locket in the cave by the sea, discovering that Regulus Black had tried to destroy it and nearly died in the process.
Harry watched Dumbledore drink the cursed potion in the cave to reach the Horcruxe locket, the potion causing Dumbledore terrible pain and weakening him significantly.
Harry dueled Inferi in the freezing waters of the cave when they attacked after Dumbledore was weakened, using fire and magical protection to keep them at bay.
Draco Malfoy was assigned by Voldemort to kill Dumbledore as punishment for his father Lucius's failure, a task that Draco desperately wanted to avoid but felt forced to complete.
Harry dated Ginny Weasley after they shared their first kiss in the Gryffindor common room, beginning a relationship that had been building since their first meeting.
Bellatrix Lestrange tortured Hermione and Ginny with the Cruciatus Curse when Death Eaters infiltrated Hogwarts through the Room of Requirement during the battle.
Harry discovered through Slughorn's true memory that Voldemort had originally created six Horcruxes, making him more vulnerable than ever if they could all be found and destroyed.
Katie Bell was cursed by an Opal Necklace that Draco Malfoy had been tasked to deliver to Dumbledore, the curse causing her to nearly die in the Room of Requirement.
Ron was poisoned by the cursed necklace that Draco had left in the enchanted cabinet, nearly dying before Harry and Hermione got him to Professor Slughorn for the antidote.
Dumbledore returned from the cave weakened and was killed by the Killing Curse cast by Snape on the Astronomy Tower, falling from the tower in a devastating moment.
Snape revealed he was the Half-Blood Prince, admitting he had written all the annotations in the textbook that had helped Harry so much throughout the year.
Snape told Harry as he fled that he had indeed loved Harry's mother Lily, and that his hatred of James was the only thing that made him dislike Harry.
Harry chased Snape through the Hogwarts corridors and classrooms, finally watching him escape through the Vanishing Cabinet in the Room of Requirement.
Regulus Black discovered the locket Horcruxe was a fake when Kreacher was sent to destroy it, and he died trying to replace it with a fake to fool Voldemort.
Kreacher returned to 12 Grimmauld Place with the fake Horcruxe locket that Regulus had left behind, a treasure the Black family kept hidden for years.
Harry received the Deluminator from Dumbledore's will, a device that could light up darkness but also connected Ron to Harry and Hermione through the communication it enabled.
The Mark of the Dark Lord glowed green above the Astronomy Tower as Dumbledore's body fell, visible to everyone in the surrounding countryside.
Harry, Ron, and Hermione left the Dursleys' house forever on the back of the flying Ford Anglia, beginning their hunt for Voldemort's Horcruxes without a plan.
The seven Harry Potters were flown from Privet Drive under heavy protection by the Order of the Phoenix, each surrounded by supporting cast members on broomsticks and sidecars.
Mad-Eye Moody was killed by Voldemort during the Seven Potters battle when his Polyjuiced body was the one that Voldemort's Killing Curse actually found.
Harry learned about the three brother story from Xenophilius Lovegood and realized he possessed the Resurrection Stone in the snitch Dumbledore left him, the second Hallow.
The Resurrection Stone brought back Harry's parents, Sirius Black, and Remus Lupin as spectral figures when Harry turned it in his hand, giving him comfort before his final task.
Harry was hit by a Killing Curse meant for him in the Forest of Dean when Voldemort tried to kill him, but the love protection from his mother saved him again.
Harry woke up in a King's Cross station-like limbo after being hit by Voldemort's Killing Curse, where he met and spoke with Dumbledore about his choice to live or die.
Voldemort's snake Nagini was revealed to be a Horcruxe when Hermione identified her as being in a protective enchanted cage wherever they went with Voldemort.
Ron destroyed Helga Hufflepuff's cup Horcruxe in the Chamber of Secrets with a Basilisk fang that Hermione had kept from the second year events.
Harry visited Godric's Hollow on Christmas Day 1997 and encountered the protective enchantments that his parents' sacrifice had left around the village to protect Harry.
Harry's parents' graves in Godric's Hollow bore the inscription 'The last enemy that shall be destroyed is death,' showing their faith that Harry would defeat Voldemort.
Hermione obliviated her parents' memories of her to protect them during the war, making them believe they were Wendell and Monica Wilkins, ordinary people with no magical children.
Harry inherited the Elder Wand from Dumbledore after Draco Malfoy disarmed Dumbledore on the Astronomy Tower, the most powerful wand in history recognizing Harry as its true master.
The golden trio infiltrated Gringotts Bank with the help of Griphook the goblin to break into Bellatrix Lestrange's vault and steal Helga Hufflepuff's Horcruxe cup.
Bellatrix Lestrange threw a knife at Hermione and cut her face when the trio was caught at the Malfoy Manor, leaving her with a permanent slash across her cheek.
Dobby the house-elf died saving Harry, Ron, and Hermione from being imprisoned in Malfoy Manor, taking Bellatrix's knife meant for them in his chest and dying in Harry's arms.
Harry buried Dobby with his own hands in the garden of Shell Cottage, placing the house-elf's simple grave marked with a rock carved with just the name Dobby.
Neville Longbottom killed Nagini the Horcruxe snake in the Room of Requirement during the Battle of Hogwarts, using the Sword of Gryffindor that came to him when he needed it.
The Battle of Hogwarts began when Voldemort and his Death Eaters attacked the castle, with students and Order members fighting to protect Hogwarts and all they held dear.
Fred Weasley died during the Battle of Hogwarts when an explosion from a wall collapsed on him, killed fighting his old enemy with his brother George at his side.
Remus Lupin and Tonks were both killed during the Battle of Hogwarts, leaving their infant son Teddy in the care of his grandmother Andromeda.
Colin Creevey returned to Hogwarts during the Battle of Hogwarts despite being underage, wanting to fight for his friends and dying during the final battle.
Harry revealed to Voldemort that he was the true master of the Elder Wand and that Draco Malfoy had won its allegiance when he disarmed Dumbledore on the tower.
Voldemort's Killing Curse rebounded and killed him when he tried to kill Harry in the Great Hall, his own magic destroying him completely as all his Horcruxes were gone.
Harry and the survivors walked out of the Forest into the Great Hall to celebrate victory, with Ron and Hermione on either side of him as the battle ended.
Nineteen years later Harry sent his own children to Hogwarts Platform Nine and Three-Quarters, watching them disappear through the barrier just as his parents once watched him.
The Thestral pulled the Gringotts cart through the ancient tunnels deep underground, taking the trio to Bellatrix Lestrange's vault guarded by the dragon they released to escape.
Luna Lovegood's father Xenophilius was kidnapped by Death Eaters after they discovered he was helping Harry and the golden trio escape their capture.
Severus Snape gave Dumbledore his memories in his will, entrusting him with the truth about Snape's love for Lily and his role as a double agent.
Harry learned through Snape's memories that Snape had been loyal to Dumbledore and to Lily's memory all along, protecting Harry for seventeen years.
Molly Weasley killed Bellatrix Lestrange in the Great Hall during the final battle, saving her daughter Ginny from the Dark witch's killing curse.
The Deathly Hallows were three magical objects: the Elder Wand, Resurrection Stone, and Cloak of Invisibility, which together made their owner the Master of Death.
Harry broke the Elder Wand after defeating Voldemort, returning it to Dumbledore's tomb and refusing to keep its power for himself.
The golden snitch that Dumbledore left Harry contained the Resurrection Stone, which Harry released by catching it with his mouth as he had caught the first snitch.
Gregorovitch had stolen the Elder Wand from Grindelwald after he saw him stealing it, and Voldemort had already killed him years before Harry learned this.
Harry's original wand was holly with a phoenix feather core from the same phoenix that gave Voldemort his wand, creating their magical Priori Incantatem connection.
Ron used the Deluminator Dumbledore left him to find Harry and Hermione when he left them during the Horcruxe hunt, the device clicking when they were nearby.

Ron Weasley observed that harry potter discovered he was a wizard when rubeus hagrid burst through the door of the dursleys' cabin on privet drive, smashing it off its hinges, and handed him his hogwarts acceptance letter on his eleventh birthday.

Hermione remembered when harry received his first wand from ollivander's shop on diagon alley - eleven inches, holly and phoenix feather, the same wand that gave voldemort his power seventeen years earlier.

Later, Harry reflected on how the goblin griphook led harry through the hidden vaults of gringotts bank, including the spectacular vault containing the philosopher's stone, protected by numerous enchantments.
Ron Weasley observed that harry met draco malfoy on the hogwarts express, who tried to befriend him but harry refused after draco insulted hagrid and mocked ron weasley.
Hermione remembered when the sorting hat was placed on harry's head and debated between gryffindor and slytherin before harry begged not to be put in slytherin, and the hat placed him in gryffindor.
Later, Harry reflected on how hermione granger corrected professor snape when he mispronounced a potion ingredient, earning his instant and lasting enmity throughout their school years.

Ron Weasley observed that neville longbottom's remembrall flashed bright red when he couldn't remember where he left his password remembrall, showing his famous forgetful nature from the very first day.

Hermione remembered when quirinus quirrell caught harry, ron, and hermione out of bed on halloween night when a troll had somehow entered the castle, though professor quirrell claimed he had already dealt with it.
Later, Harry reflected on how harry caught his first golden snitch in his mouth during his first quidditch match against draco malfoy, earning him a spot on the gryffindor quidditch team as seeker.

Ron Weasley observed that harry's firebolt broomstick was confiscated by professor mcgonagall after arriving mysteriously without a note, causing hermione to suspiciously report it to professor mcgonagall.

Hermione remembered when hermione convinced professor mcgonagall to return harry's firebolt after harry, ron, and herself discovered that norbert the norwegian ridgeback was a dragon being secretly raised in hagrid's hut.

Later, Harry reflected on how charlie weasley and his friends flew in from romania in the middle of the night to take norbert the dragon, and the three friends barely made it back to the tower before being caught.
Ron Weasley observed that hermione granger punched draco malfoy in the face when he called ron a stupid git after ron's lost rat scabbers was found to be alive.
Hermione remembered when argus filch caught harry, ron, and hermione outside the library corridor when they were searching for nicolas flamel's identity, dragging them to professor mcgonagall.

Later, Harry reflected on how harry received an invisibility cloak from dumbledore on christmas night, a gift that had belonged to harry's father james, with a note saying 'i thought you might like to use it.'

Ron Weasley observed that harry discovered the mirror of erised hidden behind a curtain in a room on the forbidden third floor, seeing his dead parents and whole family reflected back at him whenever he stood before it.
Hermione remembered when harry found nicolas flamel after dumbledore let him work out the riddle from the envelope hermione received about the philosopher's stone and his chocolate frog card.

Later, Harry reflected on how hermione granger solved the potions puzzle on the way to the philosopher's stone by correctly identifying that the second bottle contained poison while the third bottle would only allow one person forward.

Ron Weasley observed that harry confronted professor quirrell in the final chamber, discovering that quirrell was the one trying to steal the philosopher's stone and that his turban actually concealed voldemort's face.

Hermione remembered when voldemort tried to possess harry's body to reach the philosopher's stone, but harry's love and mother's sacrifice protected him, causing voldemort's essence to be destroyed and quirrell to crumble to dust.

Later, Harry reflected on how harry awoke in the hospital wing after being knocked unconscious by voldemort, and professor dumbledore revealed the mirror of erised's true nature and that only someone wanting to find but not use the stone would get it.
Ron Weasley observed that the weasley twins enchanted the passwords to the dormitories to change every saturday, and fred and george showed harry all the secret passages they had discovered.
Hermione remembered when voldemort asked professor quirrell to help him get the philosopher's stone in exchange for allowing him to teach at hogwarts, turning quirrell into his willing servant.
Later, Harry reflected on how lily evans blocked voldemort three times when he tried to kill harry, her love creating a protection that followed harry even to the dursleys' house.
Ron Weasley observed that harry first stepped onto hogwarts grounds when hagrid rowed him across the lake on the night before sorting, seeing the castle lit up against the dark sky.
Hermione remembered when the hogwarts express departed from platform nine and three-quarters at precisely 11 am on september 1st, carrying harry to his new life as a wizard.
Later, Harry reflected on how dobby appeared to harry in his bedroom at the dursleys' house, warning him about returning to hogwarts and punishing himself by slamming his head against the wall.

Ron Weasley observed that harry's scar burned whenever voldemort was feeling strong emotions, including during the confrontation with quirrell when his scar was described as feeling like it was split open.
Hermione remembered when nicolas flamel was the ancient alchemist who created the philosopher's stone, allowing him and dumbledore to live for over six hundred years before the stone was destroyed.

Later, Harry reflected on how florean fortescue gave harry extra ice cream whenever he visited diagon alley, showing kindness to the young wizard and later disappearing under mysterious circumstances.
Ron Weasley observed that the troll that entered hogwarts on halloween was actually let in by quirrell as part of voldemort's plan to distract the teachers while he went for the stone.
Hermione remembered when harry saw his parents in the mirror of erised every time he looked into it, a vision that gave him both comfort and deep sadness about the family he had lost.
Later, Harry reflected on how professor mcgonagall turned into a cat on the first day of class to demonstrate transfiguration, surprising all the first-years including harry.
Ron Weasley observed that hagrid accidentally let slip that fluffy the three-headed dog was guarding something important, giving harry and his friends a clue about the stone's protection.
Hermione remembered when neville won ten points for gryffindor by standing up to harry, ron, and hermione when they were breaking rules, showing his bravery despite his clumsiness.
Later, Harry reflected on how the devil's snare plant in the underground chamber nearly strangled harry and hermione until hermione remembered that the plant disliked fire and freed them.
Ron Weasley observed that the mirror of erised showed people their deepest desires, and only someone who wanted to find but not use the stone could obtain it from the mirror's depths.
Hermione remembered when harry and ron became best friends on the hogwarts express when ron shared his sandwiches with harry and they defeated the troll together in the bathroom.
Later, Harry reflected on how hermione became friends with harry and ron after they saved her from the troll in the bathroom, proving herself as a loyal companion despite her know-it-all attitude.
Ron Weasley observed that dobby the house-elf came to warn harry about the chamber of secrets being opened and was severely punished by the malfoys for speaking against his masters.
Hermione remembered when the potion created by nicolas flamel could turn any metal into gold and produce the elixir of life, extending the drinker's lifespan indefinitely.
Later, Harry reflected on how harry was the only gryffindor seeker in a century, a remarkable achievement that made the team competitive in the house quidditch cup.
Ron Weasley observed that the sorting hat warned the great hall that slytherin would help someone gain the stone, foreshadowing quirrell's betrayal and voldemort's return.
Hermione remembered when neville longbottom broke his wrist when he fell from his broomstick during quidditch practice, sent flying by madame hooch's whistle that surprised him.
Later, Harry reflected on how harry practiced flying with wood every evening before quidditch matches, learning advanced maneuvers that would help him catch the golden snitch.
Ron Weasley observed that the mirror of erised was moved to dumbledore's office after harry told him about it, placed behind a curtain where it remained for years.
Hermione remembered when voldemort's spirit fled godric's hollow after his killing curse rebounded, possessing quirrell for two years before attempting to steal the philosopher's stone.
Later, Harry reflected on how harry attended the end-of-term feast where gryffindor won the house cup with the last-minute points awarded by dumbledore for standing up to quirrell.

Ron Weasley observed that harry accidentally inflated aunt marge by blowing too much air into her when she insulted his parents during a dinner at the dursleys' house, forcing him to flee to the burrow.
Hermione remembered when ron weasley accidentally drove the bewitched ford anglia flying the weasley brothers had given him, crashing into the magical whomping willow in the dark of night.

Later, Harry reflected on how professor lockhart accidentally petrified harry when he tried to use ron's broken wand that backfired when they were attacked by cornish pixies in his defense against the dark arts class.
memories.location: Defense Against the Dark Arts Classroom, Hogwarts

memories.content: Ron Weasley observed that harry heard a mysterious voice coming from the walls of hogwarts saying 'there will be no more mudbloods at hogwarts,' a voice that only he and ron could hear while they were on their way to moaning myrtle's bathroom.

Hermione remembered when mrs. norris was petrified by a giant snake called basilisk that lived within the pipes of hogwarts, found hanging from a torch bracket by filch with a note reading 'piping petrifying pupils.'

Later, Harry reflected on how justin finch-fletchley was petrified when the basilisk came through the pipes in the corridor where filch caught harry after the writing on the wall appeared announcing the chamber of secrets was open.

Ron Weasley observed that nearly headless nick was petrified by the basilisk when he passed in front of a bathroom mirror that reflected the serpent's eyes instead of directly looking at it through the pipes.

Hermione remembered when hermione was petrified in the library when she found a piece of parchment about the chamber of secrets with a description of the basilisk that she had been researching before being frozen.

Later, Harry reflected on how colin creevey was petrified when he snuck into the school despite his muggle-born parents' worried letters, found in a corridor with his camera pointed toward something he had tried to photograph.

Ron Weasley observed that penelope clearwater was petrified while trying to hide from the monster with her boyfriend percy, found in the library corridor along with her shattered mirror showing the basilisk's reflection.

Hermione remembered when dobby revealed that the chamber of secrets had been opened fifty years ago by the heir of slytherin, and that he was being forced to prevent harry from returning to hogwarts by any means necessary.

Later, Harry reflected on how ginny weasley was being possessed by tom riddle's memory through her diary, writing in blood on the walls and slowly becoming more pale and withdrawn as the year progressed.

Ron Weasley observed that harry found the diary of tom riddle in ginny's belongings and when he was possessed by the memory inside it, he saw a vision of the original opening of the chamber of secrets fifty years ago.

Hermione remembered when moaning myrtle drowned when the basilisk emerged from the toilet she was crying in, fifty years before she guided harry to the entrance of the chamber of secrets through her bathroom.

Later, Harry reflected on how harry and ron entered the chamber of secrets through the hidden pipes after following ginny's location through parseltongue, using the beaded riddle diary to guide them to the underground lake.

Ron Weasley observed that tom riddle manifested fully from the diary and revealed he was voldemort as a teenager, explaining how he framed hagrid for opening the chamber fifty years ago when it was actually him.

Hermione remembered when fawkes the phoenix was sent by dumbledore to rescue harry in the chamber of secrets, bringing the sorting hat that contained the sword of gryffindor with a basilisk fang enchanted inside it.

Later, Harry reflected on how harry stabbed the basilisk through the roof of its mouth with the sword of gryffindor that he pulled from the sorting hat, killing the serpent and collecting several of its venomous fangs.

Ron Weasley observed that harry destroyed the horcruxe diary by stabbing it with a basilisk fang, causing ginny to be released from the diary's control and tom riddle's memory to be permanently destroyed.

Hermione remembered when harry asked dumbledore to employ dobby as a free elf at hogwarts, but lucius malfoy stormed in furious about the destroyed diary and dobby was sent away, later working at hogwarts kitchen.
Later, Harry reflected on how harry missed the hogwarts express and had to fly the enchanted ford anglia to school, crashing into the whomping willow on their arrival at the castle.

Ron Weasley observed that lockhart claimed he had written every book in the library about his own adventures, though most were exaggerated or completely fabricated versions of other wizards' achievements.
Hermione remembered when ginny weasley wrote terrible poetry about harry in her diary, revealing her deep crush on him before the diary's dark influence began to consume her personality.
Later, Harry reflected on how the whomping willow was planted in 1970s to hide the entrance to the tunnel leading from the shrieking shack to the hogwarts grounds.
Ron Weasley observed that filch searched the corridors for the heir of slytherin with his cat mrs. norris, growing increasingly paranoid about mudbloods being in danger.
Hermione remembered when moaning myrtle complained about being excluded and cried in her bathroom for years before harry and ron discovered she had been the first victim of the basilisk.
Later, Harry reflected on how ginny weasley was dragged into the chamber of secrets by the pull of the diary, her consciousness fading as tom riddle gained more power.
Ron Weasley observed that harry used parseltongue to open the chamber of secrets sink in moaning myrtle's bathroom, commanding the ancient snake-themed pipes to reveal the entrance.
Hermione remembered when tom riddle asked harry how he planned to stop him without using the sword, confident that harry would be unable to destroy him with the basilisk already gone.
Later, Harry reflected on how the sword of gryffindor was stored inside the sorting hat and could only be retrieved by a true gryffindor when they truly needed it.
Ron Weasley observed that fawkes's tears could heal wounds as well as his flames could burn, and his appearance signaled that dumbledore was watching over harry's safety.
Hermione remembered when ginny recovered completely once the diary was destroyed, her personality returning to normal as tom riddle's possession was lifted from her mind.
Later, Harry reflected on how lucius malfoy planted the diary in ginny's belongings hoping it would reopen the chamber and cause chaos, though he didn't know it was a horcruxe.
Ron Weasley observed that percy weasley proved his loyalty to authority by reporting his own brothers to professor mcgonagall when he suspected them of involvement in the reopening of the chamber.
Hermione remembered when harry used the marauder's map for the first time to navigate the secret passages of hogwarts, discovering hidden routes that would become essential to his adventures.
Later, Harry reflected on how fred and george weasley gave harry and ron the marauder's map that they had borrowed from filch's office, having discovered its secret phrases to activate it.
Ron Weasley observed that harry met moaning myrtle properly for the first time when he visited her bathroom to investigate the voice he had been hearing about mudbloods.
Hermione remembered when the enchanted car rescued harry and ron from the frozen lake in the forbidden forest, driving itself back to the weasley home with the unconscious boys inside.
Later, Harry reflected on how harry was caught by filch near the writing on the wall, accused of being the heir of slytherin until professor mcgonagall intervened on his behalf.
Ron Weasley observed that harry found that he could speak parseltongue because a fragment of voldemort's soul was inside him from the night his parents were killed.
Hermione remembered when the basilisk had been living in the pipes of hogwarts for a thousand years, emerging only when the heir of slytherin commanded it to purge the school of muggle-borns.

Later, Harry reflected on how harry discovered he was illegally expelled from hogwarts when the ministry sent a howler demanding he be allowed back to school immediately after uncle vernon refused to sign the hogsmeade permission form.

Ron Weasley observed that harry learned that his godfather sirius black had allegedly betrayed his parents to voldemort and murdered twelve muggles, escaping from azkaban where the dementors guarded him.
Hermione remembered when harry's firebolt was inspected by madam hooch and later cleared by ollivander who confirmed it had no dark magic curses despite hermione's concerns about its safety.

Later, Harry reflected on how professor trelawney predicted harry's death during her first divination class, claiming that the dark servant would return before the night ended and that harry would 'cross paths with death.'
Ron Weasley observed that harry's nimbus two thousand was destroyed when the knight bus rammed into the whomping willow while swerving to avoid hitting harry on his way to hogsmeade.
Hermione remembered when harry discovered the marauder's map in his birthday present from fred and george weasley, a magical map showing everyone in hogwarts and all its secret passages.
Later, Harry reflected on how hermione signed up for every possible class at hogwarts including time-turner lessons, creating a schedule so packed she barely had time to eat or sleep.
Ron Weasley observed that harry saw sirius black enter the honeydukes basement through the whomping willow's passage and tried to chase him through the tunnel to hogsmeade.
Hermione remembered when scabbers the rat bit peter pettigrew disguised as ron weasley and escaped, leading to ron's devastating loss of his beloved pet rat of twelve years.

Later, Harry reflected on how the fat lady portrait was slashed and ruined when sirius black apparently broke into the castle looking for something, causing enhanced security measures including filch's new patrol routes.
Ron Weasley observed that harry received the invisibility cloak from dumbledore at the end of last year with a note saying to use it well, which he now used to explore the castle at night.
Harry first stepped onto Hogwarts grounds when Hagrid rowed him across the lake on the night before Sorting, seeing the castle lit up against the dark sky.
The Hogwarts Express departed from Platform Nine and Three-Quarters at precisely 11 AM on September 1st, carrying Harry to his new life as a wizard.
Ginny Weasley screamed when she saw the writing on the walls and the petrified cat, knowing somehow that the diary she had been writing in was somehow responsible.
The Knight Bus slammed into the Whomping Willow while trying to avoid hitting Harry, the impact shattering windows and scattering luggage everywhere.
The champions' names burned in fire above the Goblet of Fire when Harry's name emerged as the fourth champion, shocking everyone present.
The Order of the Phoenix moved Harry from Privet Drive under the cover of magic, creating seven identical Harrys to fly in different directions.
The Mark of the Dark Lord glowed green above the Astronomy Tower as Dumbledore's body fell, visible to everyone in the surrounding countryside.
Nineteen years later, Harry stood at King's Cross with his own children, watching them board the Hogwarts Express just as he had done so long ago.
Harry discovered he could speak Parseltongue because a fragment of Voldemort's soul was inside him from the night his parents were killed.
Harry remembered that harry discovered he could speak parseltongue because a fragment of voldemort's soul was inside him from the night his parents were killed.
Ron later recounted that harry discovered he could speak parseltongue because a fragment of voldemort's soul was inside him from the night his parents were killed.
Hermione noted that harry discovered he could speak parseltongue because a fragment of voldemort's soul was inside him from the night his parents were killed.
The enchanted Ford Anglia rescued Harry and Ron from the frozen lake in the Forbidden Forest, driving itself back to the Weasley home with the unconscious boys inside.
Ron later recounted that the enchanted ford anglia rescued harry and ron from the frozen lake in the forbidden forest, driving itself back to the weasley home with the unconscious boys inside.
Hermione noted that the enchanted ford anglia rescued harry and ron from the frozen lake in the forbidden forest, driving itself back to the weasley home with the unconscious boys inside.
Everyone agreed that the enchanted ford anglia rescued harry and ron from the frozen lake in the forbidden forest, driving itself back to the weasley home with the unconscious boys inside.
Filch searched the corridors for the Heir of Slytherin with his cat Mrs. Norris, growing increasingly paranoid about Mudbloods being in danger.
Hermione noted that filch searched the corridors for the heir of slytherin with his cat mrs. norris, growing increasingly paranoid about mudbloods being in danger.
Everyone agreed that filch searched the corridors for the heir of slytherin with his cat mrs. norris, growing increasingly paranoid about mudbloods being in danger.
The story spread that filch searched the corridors for the heir of slytherin with his cat mrs. norris, growing increasingly paranoid about mudbloods being in danger.
Moaning Myrtle complained about being excluded and cried in her bathroom for years before Harry and Ron discovered she had been the first victim of the Basilisk.
Everyone agreed that moaning myrtle complained about being excluded and cried in her bathroom for years before harry and ron discovered she had been the first victim of the basilisk.
The story spread that moaning myrtle complained about being excluded and cried in her bathroom for years before harry and ron discovered she had been the first victim of the basilisk.
Later, Harry thought about when moaning myrtle complained about being excluded and cried in her bathroom for years before harry and ron discovered she had been the first victim of the basilisk.
Ginny Weasley was dragged into the Chamber of Secrets by the pull of the diary, her consciousness fading as Tom Riddle gained more power.
The story spread that ginny weasley was dragged into the chamber of secrets by the pull of the diary, her consciousness fading as tom riddle gained more power.
Later, Harry thought about when ginny weasley was dragged into the chamber of secrets by the pull of the diary, her consciousness fading as tom riddle gained more power.
Fred recalled that ginny weasley was dragged into the chamber of secrets by the pull of the diary, her consciousness fading as tom riddle gained more power.
Harry used Parseltongue to open the Chamber of Secrets sink in Moaning Myrtle's bathroom, commanding the ancient snake-themed pipes to reveal the entrance.
Later, Harry thought about when harry used parseltongue to open the chamber of secrets sink in moaning myrtle's bathroom, commanding the ancient snake-themed pipes to reveal the entrance.
Fred recalled that harry used parseltongue to open the chamber of secrets sink in moaning myrtle's bathroom, commanding the ancient snake-themed pipes to reveal the entrance.
Ginny mentioned that harry used parseltongue to open the chamber of secrets sink in moaning myrtle's bathroom, commanding the ancient snake-themed pipes to reveal the entrance.
Tom Riddle asked Harry how he planned to stop him without using the sword, confident that Harry would be unable to destroy him with the Basilisk already gone.
Fred recalled that tom riddle asked harry how he planned to stop him without using the sword, confident that harry would be unable to destroy him with the basilisk already gone.
Ginny mentioned that tom riddle asked harry how he planned to stop him without using the sword, confident that harry would be unable to destroy him with the basilisk already gone.
Neville realized that tom riddle asked harry how he planned to stop him without using the sword, confident that harry would be unable to destroy him with the basilisk already gone.
The Sword of Gryffindor was stored inside the Sorting Hat and could only be retrieved by a true Gryffindor when they truly needed it.
Ginny mentioned that the sword of gryffindor was stored inside the sorting hat and could only be retrieved by a true gryffindor when they truly needed it.
Neville realized that the sword of gryffindor was stored inside the sorting hat and could only be retrieved by a true gryffindor when they truly needed it.
Luna believed that the sword of gryffindor was stored inside the sorting hat and could only be retrieved by a true gryffindor when they truly needed it.
Fawkes's tears could heal wounds as well as his flames could burn, and his appearance signaled that Dumbledore was watching over Harry's safety.
Neville realized that fawkes's tears could heal wounds as well as his flames could burn, and his appearance signaled that dumbledore was watching over harry's safety.
Luna believed that fawkes's tears could heal wounds as well as his flames could burn, and his appearance signaled that dumbledore was watching over harry's safety.
Harry remembered that fawkes's tears could heal wounds as well as his flames could burn, and his appearance signaled that dumbledore was watching over harry's safety.
Ginny recovered completely once the diary was destroyed, her personality returning to normal as Tom Riddle's possession was lifted from her mind.
Luna believed that ginny recovered completely once the diary was destroyed, her personality returning to normal as tom riddle's possession was lifted from her mind.
Harry remembered that ginny recovered completely once the diary was destroyed, her personality returning to normal as tom riddle's possession was lifted from her mind.
Ron later recounted that ginny recovered completely once the diary was destroyed, her personality returning to normal as tom riddle's possession was lifted from her mind.
Lucius Malfoy planted the diary in Ginny's belongings hoping it would reopen the Chamber and cause chaos, though he didn't know it was a Horcruxe.
Harry remembered that lucius malfoy planted the diary in ginny's belongings hoping it would reopen the chamber and cause chaos, though he didn't know it was a horcruxe.
Ron later recounted that lucius malfoy planted the diary in ginny's belongings hoping it would reopen the chamber and cause chaos, though he didn't know it was a horcruxe.
Hermione noted that lucius malfoy planted the diary in ginny's belongings hoping it would reopen the chamber and cause chaos, though he didn't know it was a horcruxe.
Percy Weasley proved his loyalty to authority by reporting his own brothers to Professor McGonagall when he suspected them of involvement in the reopening of the Chamber.
Ron later recounted that percy weasley proved his loyalty to authority by reporting his own brothers to professor mcgonagall when he suspected them of involvement in the reopening of the chamber.
Hermione noted that percy weasley proved his loyalty to authority by reporting his own brothers to professor mcgonagall when he suspected them of involvement in the reopening of the chamber.
Everyone agreed that percy weasley proved his loyalty to authority by reporting his own brothers to professor mcgonagall when he suspected them of involvement in the reopening of the chamber.
Harry used the Marauder's Map for the first time to navigate the secret passages of Hogwarts, discovering hidden routes that would become essential to his adventures.
Hermione noted that harry used the marauder's map for the first time to navigate the secret passages of hogwarts, discovering hidden routes that would become essential to his adventures.
Everyone agreed that harry used the marauder's map for the first time to navigate the secret passages of hogwarts, discovering hidden routes that would become essential to his adventures.
